item.title: A happy dog meme dump that wasn't stopped by my internet crapping out
item.description: I'm trying to help my friend pull his little lady out of some serious kidney issues stemming from a poisoning she went through a couple years ago. The fight has been long and hard but we still hope they can get out of them in the end. If you can help them out, please do! https://gofund.me/d8e3dd17
item.dc:creator: thedeadredhead

item.title: A day in the life
item.dc:creator: ruste

item.title: Suicide sucks.
item.description: We had just seen him a couple hours before, they had aIl come over and we had grilled food and watched one of the Spiderman movies the 3 year-old likes... I know he was as much a victim as anyone else in this, but I keep having to stop myself from getting angry at him. "How dare he leave his stay at home wife and kids like this!" I think. And then I pull myself back, get angry at myself for being an asshole, then get sad, because of how little I can truly do for his wife and kids. I just... I wish there was some indication he was planning this. We knew he was struggling with depression, but I don't think any of us had any indication that he was that close to the edge. I don't know what is going to happen to the rest of his family. His wife's parents are with her now. But I worry. Rest well, my friend, may whatever demons pursued you in life cease their attack in death. They were the best neighbors we have had. Our previous neighbors were christo-facists and before that my wife and I think it was a meth lab of some sort. Aside: I think I used the wrong meme image here, but the bear's face matches my mood.
item.dc:creator: DarthMatta

item.title: The mural I painted in my living room..
item.description: I decided I wanted my house to make me smile, so I painted this mural a little while back. I have more murals planned for my house. Edit- Thanks for the love, everyone! If you’re interested in seeing more of my art (what little I have posted so far), I’m on Instagram as GnorknikArt. Also, the rug is from Wayfair.
item.dc:creator: Gnorknik

item.title: “Transvestigator” sees transwomen everywhere. Thinks Rihanna being pregnant was fake and she’s secretly trans.
item.description: Transvestigators think all the women in her pasts and most celebs online are trans. It’s a new conspiracy where instead of all celebrities secretly being part of a secret order, being Jewish, or part of a child trafficking ring, now most celebs are trans. Apparently they can’t watch TV without examining the muscle structure of every actor in the show. https://twitter.com/mandolakes/status/1544369024913457155?s=21&t=pqcSO7kfZitHnfZsvvhXyQ
item.dc:creator: tfon12

item.title: Nautilus Live Deep Sea Sightings July 5st, 2022
item.description:
Some clips from the Nautilus Live stream Today/Tomorrow/Yesterday/Time zones are hard. This is a sea cucumber being sexually tortured with a vacuum cleaner. It's okay, they love it.
Beautiful black anemone. God I wish I was a naughty piece of marine snow.
I am not enjoying Nautilus Live streams, nor relaying them here. The time zone difference and their operating schema means that I am usually screening through buffered video the next day when I wake up, instead of live at the very moment of discovery.
Prequel episode to the header video. I was very much screaming in chaotic confusion and excitement during this. Really expected that boi to be eviscerated from the suction and his gelatinous structure. Truly sea cucumbers are more fuckable than we really know. Thank you for coming to my Ted Talk.
This is a sea cucumber again, cosplaying as a reaper from Mass Effect. Another reason I am not enjoying this anymore is that it is a constant reminder that I am a failure. Sometimes a discussion happens that I have relevant input on, but I cannot contribute. Because I am human garbage.
This one is interesting for historical reasons. I mean, that is a weird sponge, whatever it is. But also they mentioned encrusting sponges, and once a few years ago the D2 on Okeanos saw fields, FIELDS of black encrusting sponges, except they weren't encrusting at all. They were like spiderwebs, meters across, everywhere. It haunts me. And no one talks about it, like it never happened. But it did, I have pictures and video. And I'm sure I'll be assassinated now for revealing this secret information, which is my goal all along. Silence me, illuminaughty daddy.
Beautiful and innocent. I would slaughter an entire abattoir of Ohioans for a single selfie with one.
A trigger fish and its silly swimming. Apparently there was another fish in stream for a moment with a HORRIBLY offensive common name (not the trigger fish), and it took a lot of convincing and citation to prove it to the current watch.
This is extremely rare, a snipe eel.
Unmute to hear scientists/engineers berate a fish by accident.
It's hard to exist when I was so determined and desperate to accomplish things, to end up a complete trash failure. Right now family are paying for me to survive with their own, limited, retirement money. So every day I don't kill myself, I'm killing my family. This crinoid, by the way, is slightly off color and strange. I may be colorblind but in effect I am a blue spectrophotometer. I see plenty of crinoid and/or blue/anti-blue stuff, but this is very definitively a strange blue. And probably it's just a hilariously common, so-boring-that-we-don't-even-talk-about-it, crinoid.
One of the geologists explaining these basalt columns. Or it could be one of the engineers who has just picked up on the visual demands of the geologists and biologists. I think this is Brandon Scott, credited as "scientist" only on the website. Forgive me, Brandon, if that isn't you.
If you want to be involved here, you could always be independently wealthy, with no debt and no student loans, and also it would help to know someone who is already involved in the project (haha nepotism but don't tell anyone). Or you could sign up to be an intern! Do you enjoy being unpaid? and being spit upon? Die, you pathetic fuck, everyone hates you. Welcome to science! You are alone in your pursuits and you are screwed if you're already not rich enough to pursue them yourself on an individual level.
If I had never discovered my love for science, I would be better off.
I could have just finished my worthless computer science degree, and gone back home to peddle meth, and gotten a meth girlfriend. We would have few teeth and a lot of love. But, I am condemned to die alone, like this.
Cat Tax. Like a true fool, I went and got 6 STEM degrees. I was endlessly curious, and I am condemned for it. Obviously I didn't just do it for nothing, I wanted to pursue a very specific neuroscience avenue which required a multidisciplinary background, and then my attempt at graduate school was absolutely.... fucked. Conned, into a fake school. Drexel's biomedical engineering "department" is a false school. Unlimited federal loans, no actual education. And the best part of all this is that many companies will just trash resumes with "Drexel" on it, if they see that. As they should, understandably, unfortunately. The only reason I got a job before was hiding my Drexel education on my resume. Thankfully I have more undergrad degrees than graduate ones. I am tired. I want to die. I will not be posting anymore dive updates from Okeanos or Nautilus Live. People don't want fun science posts, people want miserable US politics, over and over. Enjoy.
item.dc:creator: Bluefasa
